name: greycat
description: GreyCat language support for Visual Studio Code
author: Maxime Tricoire <max.tricoire@gmail.com>

enumDescriptions: No log output from the LSP server.
Everything `info` shows plus per-event traces (did_open / did_change / did_save / publish counts).
Everything `debug` shows plus full LSP request / response payloads.
greycat.trace.server.markdownDescription: Verbosity of the LSP server's log output (sets `RUST_LOG` for `greycat_analyzer_*` crates). Restart the server after changing — the extension will prompt.

greycat.lintLibs.markdownDescription: Surface lint warnings (`unused-decl`, `unused-local`, `unused-param`, `arrow-on-non-deref`) for vendored library modules under `lib/<name>/`. Off by default — those modules aren't yours and the warnings tend to drown out signal in your own code. Type-relation diagnostics always surface regardless of this setting. Restart the server after changing — the extension will prompt.

greycat.diagnosticsDebounceMs.markdownDescription: Leading-edge + trailing debounce window (ms) for analyzer-side diagnostic publishes. Within this window after the last analyzer publish, edits emit only fast diagnostics (parse / pragma / overlays + cached semantic squiggles) and schedule a trailing fire instead of re-running the analyzer on every keystroke. `0` disables debouncing (every keystroke fires the analyzer immediately). Defaults to `150`. Restart the server after changing — the extension will prompt.
